soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7573 — fix(cache): count key footprint in MokaCacheBackend eviction weight
Ali2Arslan (infrequent) · M (+49/-2, 2 files) · no escalation

S (Subjective)
  MokaCacheBackend's weigher charged only the value's size_bytes and ignored the key, so workloads storing many small values under long, high-cardinality keys under-count memory and grow past max_capacity. The fix charges a per-entry key_footprint = size_of::<InternalCacheKey>() + key.key().len() in both the eviction weigher and the synchronous approx_size_bytes, and deliberately excludes the shared prefix Arc<str> (not reclaimed per single eviction). CI green, mergeable.

O (Objective) — verified against source at head 27f0172d:
  - moka.rs:25-27 — key_footprint = size_of::<InternalCacheKey>() + key.key().len(). InternalCacheKey (backend.rs:37-41) is {prefix: Arc<str>, key: Arc<str>, type_name: &'static str}; key() returns the unique per-entry string. Correct.
  - moka.rs:49-54 — weigher now key_footprint(key).saturating_add(entry.size_bytes).try_into().unwrap_or(u32::MAX). saturating_add prevents usize overflow; unwrap_or(u32::MAX) preserves the old per-entry u32 clamp.
  - moka.rs:162-165 — approx_size_bytes sums key_footprint + size_bytes, so DeepSizeOf path and weigher use the same formula. Consistent.
  - Prefix-exclusion confirmed: build_key (mod.rs:150-152) does prefix.clone() (shared Arc) + Arc::from(key) (fresh per-entry alloc); with_key_prefix stores one shared prefix per handle. Charging prefix.len() per entry would over-count — exclusion is correct.
  - Weight source: insert_with_id sets size = cache_entry_size(&*metadata), so the weigher yields exactly key_footprint + cache_entry_size(value) — matching test_cache_weighs_key_footprint.
  - Tests: test_cache_weighs_key_footprint uses with_capacity(usize::MAX) and asserts exact weighted size; test_cache_shared_prefix_not_charged_per_entry confirms a 10KB shared prefix × 100 entries is not multiplied. Pre-existing test_cache_bytes asserts size_bytes() <= capacity (a bound) — still holds.
  - Repo sweep: MokaCacheBackend is the only production CacheBackend impl; HashMapBackend/SerializingBackend are test-only. No test asserts an exact size_bytes(). Only user-facing budgets are session.rs:109-110 (index_cache_size/metadata_cache_size).
  - could not read: none (0 omitted).

A (Assessment)
  risk: low (down from baseline med) — self-contained accounting change in one backend, no unsafe, no wire/format change, no new panic path (narrowing conversions guarded). Formula correct; the two accounting paths kept in lockstep. urgency: low. escalation: none.
  One behavioral shift: every entry now weighs ~48 bytes + key-len more, so a byte-configured cache holds slightly fewer value-bytes. Intended, negligible (<0.1%) against multi-KB entries. Worth a note for anyone who empirically tuned index_cache_size, not gating.

P (Plan)
  Clean, well-motivated, well-tested fix. No blocking conditions.

RECOMMENDATION: approve
  confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if some production consumer read size_bytes()/weighted_size() as an exact value-byte total rather than an eviction weight — the sweep found no such consumer.
summary: Correct, well-motivated fix. The weigher and approx_size_bytes now both charge key_footprint = size_of::<InternalCacheKey>() + key.key().len() and stay in lockstep; the prefix exclusion is right (shared Arc<str> cloned in build_key, not reclaimed per eviction). saturating_add + unwrap_or(u32::MAX) guard the narrowing. Two new tests pin both the key charge and the prefix non-multiplication; the exact arithmetic matches cache_entry_size. Repo sweep confirms MokaCacheBackend is the only production CacheBackend impl and no test asserts an exact size_bytes(). Approve.

comments.label: praise
comments.body: praise: Weigher correctly charges key footprint with saturating_add guarding usize overflow and unwrap_or(u32::MAX) preserving the prior per-entry clamp. Excluding the shared prefix is the right call — build_key clones the prefix Arc<str> (shared per handle) and allocates a fresh key per entry, so only key is reclaimed on a single eviction.

comments.label: nit
comments.body: nit (non-blocking): approx_size_bytes reuses key_footprint, keeping it consistent with the weigher — good. Both new tests assert through the async weighted_size() path, so the approx_size_bytes/DeepSizeOf path is only covered transitively. Low-value given the shared helper, but a one-line direct assertion would close the gap.

comments.label: suggestion
comments.body: suggestion (non-blocking): Every entry now weighs ~size_of::<InternalCacheKey>() (~48 bytes) + key length more than before, so a byte-budgeted cache holds slightly fewer value-bytes. Intended and negligible for multi-KB entries, but worth a note for the user-configurable budgets in session.rs (index_cache_size/metadata_cache_size) in case anyone tuned them empirically. No change required.
